name: Pops Foster
abstract: George Murphy "Pops" Foster (May 19, 1892 – October 30, 1969) was an American jazz musician, best known for his vigorous slap bass playing of the string bass. He also played the tuba and trumpet professionally.

works.t: What Is This Thing Called Swing?

quotes.text: Now when you made the economy hall, the economy hall was high class in the Hoops, you know. When you made the economy hall you were taught, it's just like playing at a big place in New York, what's that big opera place where? Carnegie Hall. Carnegie Hall. Well the economy hall led to us. It was just like, so when you played Carnegie, you- How about the Franz Amit? Well Franz Amit was another big one. That was the hit quality. Yes, Franz Amit. All the bright people. Yeah. We could go downstairs. That's all right, there's nothing in my way.
quotes.caption: Foster recalls Carnegie Hall as a high-class venue

about_quotes.speaker: Javon Jackson
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] This is just an aside but I’m reading a book about the bassist Pops Foster... [INTERVIEWEE] Right. Like they say at some point things kind of come...
about_quotes.doc_title: Javon Jackson interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 3, 2020 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Sherwood Mangiapane
about_quotes.evidence: his bass just influenced me. I always listened for it and did whatever I could to try to sound like Pops Foster.

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: George 'Pops' Foster whom I had seen carrying a bass violin once but had never met him.

about_quotes.speaker: Captain Verne Streckfus
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] ...And he had Pops Foster and Zutty Singleton on drums.

about_quotes.speaker: Louis Barbarin
about_quotes.evidence: And the band's name was changed to Sudden Syncopator. We played on the Steamer Capital and the Island Queen of Cincinnati. Red Island and Pop Foster was with the band at that time.

about_quotes.speaker: Red Callender
about_quotes.evidence: Luis Russell was the bandleader, and in the band was Paul Barbarin, Red Allen, Charlie Holmes, and Luis Russell playing piano -- and naturally Pops.
